blocks.narrative: 15 block types covering text, media, and basic interactive elements (accordion, tabs). Rich text block includes table support. Gaps: no hotspot, no flashcard, no timeline, no iframe embed. Meets market baseline but falls short of category leaders like Rise (which has labelled graphics, sorting activities, etc.).

assessment.narrative: Three knowledge-check types (MCQ, True/False, Short Answer) plus a dedicated Assessment lesson type with question bank and Leitner-based adaptive practice. MCP enables AI-generated questions. Gaps: no matching, drag-drop, fill-in-the-blank, or hotspot questions; passing score and attempt limits not surfaced in UI. Competitive for a personal tool but well behind enterprise tools.

ai.narrative: AI features are exclusively MCP-powered (generate_lesson, generate_quiz_for_lesson, rewrite_block, rewrite_lesson, restructure_course, review_course) — no in-UI AI buttons. For users operating via Claude + MCP this is genuinely powerful; for non-technical users the AI features are invisible. Translation and image generation are absent. The approach is novel and effective within its intended workflow.

compliance.narrative: SCORM 1.2 export is present and functional. No SCORM 2004, xAPI/Tin Can, LTI, PDF export, or video export. For a tool positioning toward L&D professionals this is a meaningful gap, though SCORM 1.2 covers most LMS compatibility needs.

analytics.narrative: No analytics, completion tracking, quiz score reporting, or learner progress dashboard of any kind. This is the most significant gap relative to market expectations for an L&D authoring tool. SCORM completion tracking passes to the LMS but there is no native reporting.

collaboration.narrative: Explicitly a single-author tool with no multi-author support, commenting, version history, or review workflow. The MCP review_course tool provides AI-based content review but no human collaboration layer exists.

templates.narrative: No course or lesson templates, no theme customisation, no white-labelling, no custom domain. Course creation starts from a blank slate every time.

branching.narrative: Strictly linear navigation. No conditional branching, prerequisites, or personalised paths. Assessment lessons use adaptive question ordering (Leitner) internally but this does not constitute learner path branching.

media.narrative: Direct video and file upload to Supabase CDN works well. No stock media library, no in-app image editing, no subtitle support. Meets the basic upload/host need but offers no media management experience.

accessibility.narrative: Basic keyboard interactions work (Escape to close modals, slash commands). No formal WCAG audit, no screen reader testing documented, alt text support unconfirmed. Needs dedicated accessibility work before L&D-compliance use cases.

publishing.narrative: Shareable URL, SCORM 1.2, HTML export, and Course JSON in a single publish modal is a strong set. Validation warnings surface before publish. Gaps are password protection, SSO-gated learner access, and SCORM 2004. A notable strength relative to tool's overall maturity.

extensibility.narrative: The MCP server with 32 tools is genuinely best-in-class across the authoring tool market. No other tool exposes full CRUD over courses, lessons, and blocks plus AI semantic operations via the Model Context Protocol. The intended workflow — L&D expert + Claude + MCP — is coherent and unique. The only limitation is that non-Claude users cannot access any of this capability.

blocks.narrative: Comprehensive and fully documented block library. Text, media, interactive (accordion, tabs, hotspot, process, timeline, scenario, sorting, flashcards, buttons), assessment (MCQ, multiple response, fill-in-the-blank, matching), chart (bar, line, pie), structural (continue, divider, spacer), plus table, math, code block, and Storyline embed. Every benchmark block type is confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

assessment.narrative: Full-featured assessment system. Question types: MCQ, multiple response, fill-in-the-blank, matching. Knowledge checks (unscored) and quizzes (scored) are distinct. Question banks, randomisation, anchor questions, passing score all confirmed. AI quiz generation (3-25 questions). Evidence: [marketing|docs]

ai.narrative: Comprehensive AI suite: course generation from prompt or source document, content rewrite/tone/grammar, quiz generation, image generation (editorial/3D/cartoon/watercolor from 13M+ stock), AI voice (thousands of voices), AI captions for video, AI audio transcripts, AI timeline/table block generation. AI features on separate paid tier. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

compliance.narrative: Best-in-class: SCORM 1.2, SCORM 2004, xAPI/Tin Can, cmi5, AICC — five standards from a single publish flow. PDF export available. No LTI native. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

analytics.narrative: Quick Share: basic completion/view tracking. Reach 360: learner progress, completion, quiz scores, manual overrides. Functional but deliberately lightweight — not suited for complex enterprise reporting. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

collaboration.narrative: Real-time collaborative editing (Teams tier). Review 360: inline comments, file attachments, review requests with due dates, completion tracking, nested folders. Shared block templates and question banks. Roles: Primary Admin, Admin, Manager, Reporter, Creator, Learner. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

templates.narrative: 1,000+ course templates, 100+ for onboarding. Three built-in visual themes with extensive sub-customisation. Custom font upload, custom hex colors. Block templates saveable and shareable across Teams. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

branching.narrative: Scenario blocks support branching roleplay. Continue blocks gate progression. No conditional navigation between lessons natively. Storyline 360 embed is the workaround for full branching. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

media.narrative: Content Library 360 (13M+ stock assets), AI image generation, direct video upload, CDN hosting (150GB individual / unlimited Teams), AI captions, AI audio transcripts, AI voice/TTS. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

accessibility.narrative: WCAG 2.1 Level AA, Section 508, EN 301 549. Keyboard navigation, screen reader support (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, Narrator), alt text, auto color contrast. Known gaps tracked in public Accessibility Maturity Plan. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

publishing.narrative: Shareable link (Quick Share with analytics), Reach 360 direct publish, SCORM/xAPI/cmi5/AICC export, PDF, web publish. No confirmed password protection on Quick Share. SSO via Reach 360. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

extensibility.narrative: Reach 360 REST API and Webhooks for delivery layer. No authoring REST API. No Zapier/Make. No MCP server. SSO via Okta. Evidence: [docs]

blocks.narrative: Core media blocks plus Interactive category (flip cards, card sorting, how-to, walkthrough with hotspot overlays). No accordion, tabs, timeline, or code block confirmed. AI image generation inline. Assessment question types are notably broad (9 types). Evidence: [marketing|docs]

assessment.narrative: Broad question type set: MCQ, multiple-choice, True/False, fill-in-the-blank, text matching, drag-and-drop, ranking, hotspot image, open questions. Passing score confirmed. Question banks and randomisation not explicitly confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

ai.narrative: EasyAI 2.0: AI block generation (format auto-selection), content rewrite/tone/grammar, quiz generation (all types), EasyTranslate (75 languages), AI image generation, EasyVideo (AI avatar narration), TTS, AI subtitles. Course Guidelines for context-aware output. All plans. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

compliance.narrative: SCORM 1.2, SCORM 2004, xAPI/Tin Can, LTI 1.2, PDF export. Dynamic SCORM (update-in-place). SCORM import from Articulate Rise. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

analytics.narrative: EasyAnalytics: completion tracking, quiz scores (mastery), per-learner results, CSV export. Per-course reporting only — no unified cross-course dashboard. Full analytics gated to Team/Enterprise. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

collaboration.narrative: Real-time co-authoring (Team/Enterprise). External reviewer links (no account required, optional password). Page-level comments, @mentions, notifications. Organisations feature with roles and groups. Version history not confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

templates.narrative: Hundreds of ready-to-use course templates in Course Library. Branded theme system (logo, colours, fonts) with save-as-theme. Template lock for organisational consistency. No white-label confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

branching.narrative: No in-course branching or conditional navigation confirmed. Linear-first architecture matching SME-simplicity positioning. No prerequisites or personalised paths. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

media.narrative: Video upload, CDN hosting, EasyVideo (AI avatar narration), TTS, AI image generation, AI subtitles. No built-in stock media library. No in-product image editing confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

accessibility.narrative: WCAG compliance confirmed. Screen reader support, keyboard navigation, alt text all listed. TTS and AI subtitles improve learner accessibility. Section 508 not explicitly mentioned. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

publishing.narrative: Link, QR code, embed, SCORM 1.2/2004, xAPI, LTI 1.2, HTML, PDF. Dynamic SCORM. Password protection. SSO via LTI. LXP integrations (EdCast, Degreed, HowNow, LearnUpon, BlendLXP). Evidence: [marketing|docs]

extensibility.narrative: Webhooks confirmed. API confirmed but scope limited and plan-gated. SAML 2.0 SSO for authors. 5 LXP integrations. No Zapier/Make. No MCP server. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

blocks.narrative: Core media types confirmed. Specific interactive block types (accordion, tabs, timeline, hotspot) not enumerated in public sources. 'Customizable page layouts' and 'best practice templates' suggest breadth but block catalogue is unclear without direct access. Evidence: [marketing]

assessment.narrative: MCQ, surveys, passing score, question pools/randomization, open-text feedback, interactive polling all confirmed. Fill-in-the-blank, matching, drag-drop, hotspot not confirmed. Gamification badges add engagement. Evidence: [marketing]

ai.narrative: AI course generation from source material and content enhancement (intros/summaries) confirmed. Auto-translate (75 languages + glossary). 'Elucidat Expert' AI partner coming soon. No AI image generation, no AI learner feedback, no AI tutor. Evidence: [marketing]

compliance.narrative: SCORM 1.2, SCORM 2004, xAPI/Tin Can all confirmed on all plans. Multi-language SCORM. Word export available. No LTI. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

analytics.narrative: Analytics dashboards, learner-level data (completions, scores, responses), data export confirmed. Dashboards only included by default on Enterprise — add-ons on lower tiers. Evidence: [marketing]

collaboration.narrative: Multi-author, inline review comments, external reviewers via time-limited links, comment audit trail, project locking, review workflow, custom user roles. G2 consistently praises collaboration. Evidence: [marketing|review]

templates.narrative: Ready-made templates (with inline guidance and storyboards), company templates, customizable page layouts, global brand control, custom fonts, asset library. 'Hundreds of templates' per reviewers. Evidence: [marketing|review]

branching.narrative: Branching content, personalized learning paths, branching video, content personalization using names/scores/responses all confirmed on all plans. Prerequisites not confirmed. Evidence: [marketing]

media.narrative: Video upload, YouTube/Brightcove embed, audio, TTS, screen and camera recording, Pixabay stock library, CDN hosting. In-product image editing and subtitles not confirmed. Evidence: [marketing]

accessibility.narrative: WCAG 2.1 AA, Section 508, keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, alt text, customizable colour contrast, logical content structure, readable fonts, accessibility-ready page types. Comprehensive and explicit. Evidence: [marketing]

publishing.narrative: SCORM 1.2/2004, xAPI, URL release, instant re-release, multi-language SCORM, 15+ LMS direct integrations, offline access. Best-in-class. Evidence: [marketing]

extensibility.narrative: Full REST API (Account, Projects, Authors, Releases), Webhooks, xAPI LRS API. SSO (SAML). Docebo and CrossKnowledge integrations. No Zapier/Make, no MCP. API gated to higher tiers. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

blocks.narrative: Rich text, image, video, polls, quizzes, reflection cards, wiki pages. Slide-metaphor editor. No accordion, tabs, timeline, table, flashcard, hotspot, or code block confirmed. AI-generated content structure philosophy over manual block library. Evidence: [marketing]

assessment.narrative: MCQ, quizzes, assessments, reflection cards confirmed. G2 confirms 'premade question types.' Specific types beyond MCQ not enumerated. AI tutor generates follow-up questions and personalised reviews (spaced repetition). Certificate generation confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|review]

ai.narrative: AI is the core platform differentiator: course generation from PDFs/URLs/documents, content rewrite/expand, quiz generation, translation (60+ languages), AI tutor ('Ask') for learners (24/7 Q&A, scenario practice, personalised reviews), natural language analytics. Auto-tagging of skills. Evidence: [marketing]

compliance.narrative: SCORM 1.2, SCORM 2004, xAPI, IMS LTI all confirmed. SCORM export from Sana-native content not explicitly confirmed (import is primary use case). No PDF or video export. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

analytics.narrative: Natural language analytics ('ask any question about learning performance in plain English'). Completion tracking, compliance tracking, skill auto-tagging, leaderboards. Power BI integration. Standout feature set. Evidence: [marketing]

collaboration.narrative: Real-time collaborative authoring confirmed. G2 mentions edit mode and comments but notes a UX bug. Review/approval workflow and version history not confirmed. Custom roles confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|review]

templates.narrative: Ready-to-use content library of editable courses. Custom branding (fonts, colours, logos). Theme customisation. Media library. No large numbered template library comparable to Elucidat. AI generation substitutes for templates. Evidence: [marketing]

branching.narrative: AI-personalised learning paths based on skill level confirmed. Conditional navigation implied by AI path assignment. Prerequisites not confirmed as a named feature. AI tutor provides adaptive non-linear learning. Evidence: [marketing|review]

media.narrative: Video upload, media library, CDN hosting. No stock media library, in-product image editing, or subtitle support confirmed. Strong on AI-generated content from documents but raw media handling is less documented. Evidence: [marketing]

accessibility.narrative: Section 508 confirmed. WCAG 2.1 AA not explicitly stated in Sana's marketing. ISO 27001 and SOC 2 featured but these are security not accessibility. Keyboard and screen reader support not confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

publishing.narrative: Content published within Sana's hosted LMS. SAML SSO for learner access. SCORM/xAPI/LTI for external integration. No anonymous shareable link. Branded portal. Multi-language (60+). Walled-garden appropriate for enterprise. Evidence: [marketing]

extensibility.narrative: REST API for users, groups, assignments, content, reporting. 100+ integrations. SAML SSO, SCIM. Named integrations span SSO, HRIS (40+), productivity, content libraries. No Zapier/Make, no MCP. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

blocks.narrative: Lesson slides, MCQ quizzes, flip cards, key takeaways, examples, actionable steps. Images and video. AI narration. No accordion, tabs, hotspot, timeline, table, or code block confirmed. AI-first generation model limits manual block variety. Evidence: [marketing|review]

assessment.narrative: MCQ quizzes confirmed with AI generation. Knowledge checks (key takeaways, flip cards). No true/false, fill-in-the-blank, matching, drag-drop, hotspot, essay, question banks, randomisation, or passing score confirmed. Basic relative to market. Evidence: [marketing|review]

ai.narrative: AI course generation from PDFs/videos/PPTx/web links/voice notes. AI quiz generation per lesson. AI content rewrite. AI narration (lifelike audio). AI translation (120+ languages). No AI image generation or AI learner tutor confirmed. Among the most AI-native tools. Evidence: [marketing|review]

compliance.narrative: SCORM export confirmed. PowerPoint export confirmed. xAPI, LTI, PDF export not confirmed. Functional for basic SCORM LMS delivery but lacks enterprise breadth. Evidence: [marketing|review]

analytics.narrative: No analytics dashboard confirmed in standalone product. Completion tracking available via SCORM delivery to LMS. LearnUpon integration (planned 2026) would add analytics. Evidence: [marketing]

collaboration.narrative: Real-time commenting confirmed. Multi-author implied. No structured review/approval workflow, version history, external reviewer links, or granular roles confirmed. Lightweight. Evidence: [marketing|review]

templates.narrative: AI generates structured drafts replacing traditional templates. Implicit lesson structures (key takeaways, flip cards). Theme customisation mentioned. No traditional named template library. Evidence: [marketing|review]

branching.narrative: No conditional navigation or personalised learning paths confirmed. Sequential slide/card structure. LearnUpon's Learning Journeys is a separate LMS feature. Effectively absent. Evidence: [marketing|review]

media.narrative: Video and image upload. AI narration (audio generation). No stock media library, subtitle support, or in-product image editing depth confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|review]

accessibility.narrative: WCAG 2.1 AA not confirmed. Keyboard and screen reader support not mentioned. AI translation (120+ languages) and AI narration are accessibility-adjacent but not substitutes for WCAG compliance. Evidence: [marketing]

publishing.narrative: Link, QR, SCORM, embed, PowerPoint, live embed. Password protection confirmed. No xAPI, no instant re-release, no SSO for learners. Mid-market coverage. Evidence: [marketing|review]

extensibility.narrative: No REST API, webhooks, Zapier, or Make confirmed. SCORM and link sharing are the primary integration mechanisms. LearnUpon acquisition signals future depth but current state is minimal. No MCP. Evidence: [marketing]

blocks.narrative: 20+ tile types claimed. Confirmed: text, image, accordion, flashcard, MCQ, short answer, multiple selection, sorting activity, branching scenario, knowledge check, AI role-play conversation, AI avatar video. AI conversation block is a standout differentiator. Tabs, timeline, table, code, hotspot, iframe not confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|review]

assessment.narrative: MCQ, short answer, multiple selection with conditional logic. Knowledge checks confirmed. Passing score implied by SCORM/xAPI tracking. No true/false, fill-in-the-blank, matching, drag-drop, hotspot, question banks, or randomisation confirmed. AI grading for short answers mentioned. Evidence: [marketing|review]

ai.narrative: AI is the central capability: document-to-eLearning generation, storyboard-guided prompts, content rewrite and whole-lesson transformation, quiz/knowledge-check generation, translation (70+ languages, dynamic multilingual packaging), AI image generation, AI role-play conversation tutor, AI avatar video. RAG-based generation for source grounding. Whole-lesson agent mode is a distinctive agentic capability. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

compliance.narrative: SCORM export (free tier), xAPI confirmed. Auto-updating SCORM (live connection to Mindsmith servers). Specific SCORM version not confirmed. No LTI. PDF of metrics on Business tier. Cloud dependency from auto-updating SCORM. Evidence: [marketing]

analytics.narrative: Completion rate, answer distribution, per-learner results, analytics dashboard. PDF export of metrics on Business tier. 'Actionable insights' listed as coming soon. No time-on-task, cohort analytics, CSV export, or LRS integration confirmed. Basic. Evidence: [marketing]

collaboration.narrative: Real-time editing (Business tier). Commenting and review workflows. Version history and staging (Business). Role-based permissions. Multi-workspace (Enterprise only). External reviewer links not confirmed. Thin below Business tier. Evidence: [marketing]

templates.narrative: Anti-template philosophy: AI generates contextually appropriate layouts rather than providing a template library. Custom branding (Professional+). Workspace branding (Business). White-label not confirmed. Template concept dissolved into AI generation flow. Evidence: [marketing]

branching.narrative: Branching scenarios with Experience Canvas visual editor. AI-generated branches. Conditional feedback. Up to 40-node scenarios. Prerequisites not confirmed. Microlearning format constrains scenario complexity. Above baseline. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

media.narrative: Video implied. CDN via auto-updating SCORM architecture. AI image generation. TTS narration (Eleven Labs on Business). AI avatar video. No stock library, in-product image editing, or subtitle support confirmed. AI avatar video is a differentiator. Evidence: [marketing]

accessibility.narrative: WCAG 2.2 AA claimed — most recent standard, more demanding than 2.1 AA claimed by most competitors. SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA. Keyboard and screen reader support implied by WCAG 2.2 conformance but not individually confirmed. Evidence: [marketing]

publishing.narrative: Shareable link (no account required), embed, SCORM (free), xAPI. Auto-updating SCORM is headline differentiator. Multi-language dynamic packaging (70+ languages). SCORM version not confirmed. No LTI, password protection, or video export. 2-lesson sharing cap on free. Evidence: [marketing]

extensibility.narrative: No public REST API. No webhooks. No Zapier/Make. No MCP. SAML/SSO Enterprise-only. SOC 2, GDPR, CCPA compliance. Self-contained platform below Enterprise tier. Evidence: [marketing]

strategic_tiers.gap: No analytics or learner progress tracking of any kind
strategic_tiers.rationale: Every competitor offers at least basic completion tracking. L&D professionals cannot evaluate a tool they cannot measure. Even basic completion rate and quiz score tracking on the shareable URL would move this from 0 to 2. SCORM tracking handles LMS use cases, but the shareable-link use case has zero visibility.

Every competitor except Courseau (which substitutes AI generation) offers templates. For L&D professionals, templates are the expected starting point — not a blank canvas. 3-5 templates covering common L&D use cases (onboarding, compliance, product training) would move this score from 0 to 2 and dramatically improve first-use experience.

Flashcards are present in Rise, Easygenerator (flip cards), Courseau (flip cards), and Mindsmith. They are one of the most commonly used interactive block types in e-learning. Adding a flashcard/flip card block would close a visible gap and improve the assessment lesson type with Leitner scheduling.

strategic_tiers.gap: Only 3 question types (MCQ, True/False, Short Answer) — no matching, fill-in-the-blank, or drag-drop
strategic_tiers.rationale: Easygenerator offers 9 question types; Rise offers 4 with banks and randomisation. TideLearn's 3 types are the minimum viable set. Adding fill-in-the-blank and matching would bring the score to 3 and satisfy most L&D assessment needs.

strategic_tiers.gap: No WCAG audit, no screen reader testing, accessibility posture undocumented
strategic_tiers.rationale: Accessibility is a credibility dimension for academic evaluation. Elucidat and Rise both publish WCAG conformance claims and VPATs. Mindsmith claims WCAG 2.2 AA. TideLearn's lack of any documented accessibility posture is a weakness that MA supervisors and enterprise-minded investors would flag immediately. A basic WCAG audit with documented findings and an alt text system would move this to 2-3.

strategic_tiers.gap: No SCORM 2004 or xAPI export — only SCORM 1.2
strategic_tiers.rationale: SCORM 1.2 covers most LMS compatibility but SCORM 2004 and xAPI are expected by modern L&D teams. Rise, Easygenerator, Elucidat, and Sana all support multiple standards. Adding xAPI would be the highest-leverage compliance improvement as it enables modern LRS tracking.

strategic_tiers.gap: No iframe/embed block for third-party content
strategic_tiers.rationale: Rise, Easygenerator, and Mindsmith all support iframe/embed blocks. An embed block would allow course authors to include external content (Google Forms, Padlet, H5P interactions, Loom videos, etc.) — extending TideLearn's block capabilities without building new block types.

strategic_tiers.gap: No in-app AI features visible to non-MCP users
strategic_tiers.rationale: DELIBERATE OMISSION. TideLearn's MCP-first AI strategy is a conscious architectural decision, not a gap. The target audience is Claude users who operate AI via the MCP workflow. Adding in-app AI buttons would duplicate MCP capabilities, increase maintenance surface, and dilute the product's unique positioning. Investors should understand this as a strategic choice. The risk is that non-Claude evaluators see a tool with 'no AI' and dismiss it — the mitigation is clear marketing that positions MCP as the AI layer.

strategic_tiers.gap: No multi-author support, commenting, or review workflows
strategic_tiers.rationale: DEFERRED. Multi-author collaboration is an enterprise feature that requires significant infrastructure (real-time sync, conflict resolution, role-based access). TideLearn is explicitly a single-author tool. This is appropriate for the current stage but investors should see a roadmap note acknowledging this as a future growth vector.

strategic_tiers.gap: No conditional branching or personalised learning paths
strategic_tiers.rationale: DEFERRED. Branching is a mid-to-advanced feature that even Rise only partially supports (scenario blocks only, no lesson-level branching). The field average of 2.3 confirms this is not a universal table-stakes feature. Worth noting on a long-term roadmap but not blocking for any current audience.

strategic_tiers.gap: No SCORM 2004, LTI, or multi-author workflows
strategic_tiers.rationale: DEFERRED. SCORM 2004, LTI, and multi-author are enterprise features that match enterprise sales cycles. TideLearn is not targeting enterprise sales. These are appropriate deferrals that should appear on a 12-month roadmap for investor communication but are not blocking near-term value.

strategic_tiers.gap: No stock media library or AI image generation
strategic_tiers.rationale: DEFERRED. Rise has 13M+ stock assets; Elucidat has Pixabay. Building a stock library is expensive. A lightweight alternative would be an Unsplash/Pexels API integration — this could be a quick win but is not blocking for the core authoring use case.

evidence_source_distribution.notes: Feature scores are derived from the same evidence sources as UI scores. TideLearn's feature scores reflect actual implemented capabilities (developer-owner knowledge). Competitor feature scores reflect documented/marketed capabilities and may understate actual product depth. Feature checklists with presence/unknown/no tags are included in each tool's feature-scores file for transparency.

methodology.scoring_scale: 0-5 scale for features (0 = completely absent, 1 = minimal/token presence, 2 = below market expectations, 3 = functional baseline, 4 = strong and competitive, 5 = best-in-class). Feature scores may differ from UI dimension scores because they measure depth of capability rather than design quality.

strategic_tier_definitions.table_stakes: Features that every competitor has and whose absence makes TideLearn look unfinished or not ready for real L&D work. These should be prioritised for near-term development.
strategic_tier_definitions.strategic_non_feature: Deliberate omissions that make sense given TideLearn's MCP-first positioning and target audience. These should not be built but should be explained clearly in marketing and investor materials.
strategic_tier_definitions.deferred: Enterprise or advanced features that are not needed for TideLearn's current stage and target audience. These belong on a 6-12 month roadmap for investor communication but should not distract from table-stakes work.

known_limitations: Feature depth assessments for tools without direct access (especially Sana, Elucidat) may undercount capabilities that are not publicly documented.
AI feature quality was not tested — only feature presence/absence is scored.
Field averages are computed across all 7 tools including TideLearn, which may skew averages downward for categories where TideLearn scores 0.
Compliance scores weigh standard support breadth but do not assess implementation quality or LMS compatibility testing.
